title: Peloton's Q2 FY2026 Earnings: Revenue Misses Expectations
text: Peloton Q2 FY2026 Earnings Report. Revenue Miss: Peloton reported Q2 FY2026 revenue that missed analyst expectations, indicating softness in the core equipment business. Cross Training Sales: Cross training sales were lower than expected, suggesting weakness in new product categories launched to drive growth. Connected Fitness Subscriptions: The company continued to report declining connected fitness subscriptions on a year-over-year basis. Churn Dynamics: Monthly churn rates remained elevated due to the October membership price increase and macroeconomic headwinds. Cash Flow: Despite the revenue miss, free cash flow remained positive at $71 million, with unrestricted cash and equivalents of $1.18 billion. Financial Position: The balance sheet remained strong with net debt of only $173 million, down $76 million quarter-over-quarter. Adjusted EBITDA: The company reported adjusted EBITDA of $81 million, up 39% year-over-year and $6 million above guidance. Profitability Focus: The company continued its focus on profitability and free cash flow generation despite top-line challenges.
